10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_TAG_FILTER28_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains per VLAN Tag filter control information. Offset: 0x1600054 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:29 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved. 28:25 RW 0x0 DMACHN: DMA Channel Number. The DMA Channel number to which the VLAN Tagged Packet is to be routed if it passes this VLAN Tag Filter is programmed in this field. If the Routing based on VLAN Tag Filter is not necessary, this field need not be programmed. RW 0x0 DMACHEN: DMA Channel Number Enable. This bit is the Enable for the DMA Channel Number value programmed in the field DMACH. If this is reset, then the Routing does not occur based on VLAN Filter result. The packet is routed based on DA Based DMA Channel Routing. 23:21 RO 0x0 Reserved_21: Reserved. RW 0x0 ERIVLT: Enable Inner VLAN Tag. When this bit and the EDVLP field are set, the MAC receiver enables operation on the inner VLAN Tag (if present). When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables operation on the outer VLAN Tag (if present). The ERSVLM bit determines which VLAN type is enabled for filtering or matching.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 ERSVLM: Enable Receive S-VLAN Match. When this bit is set, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for S-VLAN (Type = 0x88A8) packets. When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for CVLAN (Type = 0x8100) packets. The ERIVLT bit determines the VLAN tag position considered for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 DOVLTC: Disable VLAN Type Check. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 ETV: Enable 12-Bit VLAN Tag Comparison. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_TAG_FILTER29_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains per VLAN Tag filter control information. Offset: 0x1700054 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:29 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description 28:25 RW 0x0 DMACHN: DMA Channel Number. The DMA Channel number to which the VLAN Tagged Packet is to be routed if it passes this VLAN Tag Filter is programmed in this field. If the Routing based on VLAN Tag Filter is not necessary, this field need not be programmed. RW 0x0 DMACHEN: DMA Channel Number Enable. This bit is the Enable for the DMA Channel Number value programmed in the field DMACH. If this is reset, then the Routing does not occur based on VLAN Filter result. The packet is routed based on DA Based DMA Channel Routing. 23:21 RO 0x0 Reserved_21: Reserved. RW 0x0 ERIVLT: Enable Inner VLAN Tag. When this bit and the EDVLP field are set, the MAC receiver enables operation on the inner VLAN Tag (if present). When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables operation on the outer VLAN Tag (if present). The ERSVLM bit determines which VLAN type is enabled for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 ERSVLM: Enable Receive S-VLAN Match. When this bit is set, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for S-VLAN (Type = 0x88A8) packets. When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for CVLAN (Type = 0x8100) packets. The ERIVLT bit determines the VLAN tag position considered for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 DOVLTC: Disable VLAN Type Check. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 ETV: Enable 12-Bit VLAN Tag Comparison. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set.

When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_TAG_FILTER8_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains per VLAN Tag filter control information. Offset: 0x1f00054 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:29 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description 28:25 RW 0x0 DMACHN: DMA Channel Number. The DMA Channel number to which the VLAN Tagged Packet is to be routed if it passes this VLAN Tag Filter is programmed in this field. If the Routing based on VLAN Tag Filter is not necessary, this field need not be programmed. RW 0x0 DMACHEN: DMA Channel Number Enable. This bit is the Enable for the DMA Channel Number value programmed in the field DMACH. If this is reset, then the Routing does not occur based on VLAN Filter result. The packet is routed based on DA Based DMA Channel Routing. 23:21 RO 0x0 Reserved_21: Reserved. RW 0x0 ERIVLT: Enable Inner VLAN Tag. When this bit and the EDVLP field are set, the MAC receiver enables operation on the inner VLAN Tag (if present). When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables operation on the outer VLAN Tag (if present). The ERSVLM bit determines which VLAN type is enabled for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 ERSVLM: Enable Receive S-VLAN Match. When this bit is set, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for S-VLAN (Type = 0x88A8) packets. When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for CVLAN (Type = 0x8100) packets. The ERIVLT bit determines the VLAN tag position considered for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 DOVLTC: Disable VLAN Type Check. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 ETV: Enable 12-Bit VLAN Tag Comparison. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_TAG_FILTER9_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains per VLAN Tag filter control information. Offset: 0x2000054 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:29 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved. 28:25 RW 0x0 DMACHN: DMA Channel Number. The DMA Channel number to which the VLAN Tagged Packet is to be routed if it passes this VLAN Tag Filter is programmed in this field. If the Routing based on VLAN Tag Filter is not necessary, this field need not be programmed. RW 0x0 DMACHEN: DMA Channel Number Enable. This bit is the Enable for the DMA Channel Number value programmed in the field DMACH. If this is reset, then the Routing does not occur based on VLAN Filter result. The packet is routed based on DA Based DMA Channel Routing. 23:21 RO 0x0 Reserved_21: Reserved. RW 0x0 ERIVLT: Enable Inner VLAN Tag. When this bit and the EDVLP field are set, the MAC receiver enables operation on the inner VLAN Tag (if present). When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables operation on the outer VLAN Tag (if present). The ERSVLM bit determines which VLAN type is enabled for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 ERSVLM: Enable Receive S-VLAN Match. When this bit is set, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for S-VLAN (Type = 0x88A8) packets. When this bit is reset, the MAC receiver enables filtering or matching for CVLAN (Type = 0x8100) packets. The ERIVLT bit determines the VLAN tag position considered for filtering or matching. RW 0x0 DOVLTC: Disable VLAN Type Check. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 ETV: Enable 12-Bit VLAN Tag Comparison. When this bit is set, a 12-bit VLAN identifier is used for comparing and filtering instead of the complete 16-bit VLAN tag. Bits[11:0] of VLAN tag are compared with the corresponding field in the received VLAN-tagged packet. Similarly, when enabled, only 12 bits of the VLAN tag in the received packet are used for hash-based VLAN filtering. When this bit is reset, all 16 bits of the 15th and 16th bytes of the received VLAN packet are used for comparison and VLAN hash filtering. RW 0x0 VEN: VLAN Tag Enable. This bit is used to enable or disable the VLAN Tag. When this bit is set, the MAC compares the VLAN Tag of received packet with the VLAN Tag ID. When this bit is not set, no comparison is performed irrespective of the programming of the other fields. 15:0 RW 0x0 VID: VLAN Tag ID. This field holds the VLAN Tag value which is used by the MAC for perfect comparison. It is valid when VLAN Tag Enable is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_INCL0_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The per Tx Queue VLAN Tag Inclusion register contains the VLAN tag for insertion in the Transmit packets from respective Tx Queue. It also contains the VLAN tag insertion controls. Offset: 0x2100060 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:17 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 CSVL: C-VLAN or S-VLAN. When this bit is set, S-VLAN type (0x88A8) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. When this bit is reset, C-VLAN type (0x8100) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. 15:0 RW 0x0 VLT: VLAN Tag for Transmit Packets. This field contains the value of the VLAN tag to be inserted or replaced. The value must only be changed when the transmit lines are inactive or during the initialization phase. Bits[15:13] are the User Priority field, Bit 12 is the CFI/DEI field, and Bits[11:0] are the VID field in the VLAN tag. The following list describes the bits of this field: - Bits[15:13]: User Priority - Bit 12: Canonical Format Indicator (CFI) or Drop Eligible Indicator (DEI) - Bits[11:0]: VLAN Identifier (VID) field of VLAN tag

The per Tx Queue VLAN Tag Inclusion register contains the VLAN tag for insertion in the Transmit packets from respective Tx Queue. It also contains the VLAN tag insertion controls. Offset: 0x2f00060 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description 31:17 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 CSVL: C-VLAN or S-VLAN. When this bit is set, S-VLAN type (0x88A8) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. When this bit is reset, C-VLAN type (0x8100) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. 15:0 RW 0x0 VLT: VLAN Tag for Transmit Packets. This field contains the value of the VLAN tag to be inserted or replaced. The value must only be changed when the transmit lines are inactive or during the initialization phase. Bits[15:13] are the User Priority field, Bit 12 is the CFI/DEI field, and Bits[11:0] are the VID field in the VLAN tag. The following list describes the bits of this field: - Bits[15:13]: User Priority - Bit 12: Canonical Format Indicator (CFI) or Drop Eligible Indicator (DEI) - Bits[11:0]: VLAN Identifier (VID) field of VLAN tag MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VLAN_INCL9_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The per Tx Queue VLAN Tag Inclusion register contains the VLAN tag for insertion in the Transmit packets from respective Tx Queue. It also contains the VLAN tag insertion controls. Offset: 0x3000060 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:17 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 CSVL: C-VLAN or S-VLAN. When this bit is set, S-VLAN type (0x88A8) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. When this bit is reset, C-VLAN type (0x8100) is inserted or replaced in the 13th and 14th bytes of transmitted packets. 15:0 RW 0x0 VLT: VLAN Tag for Transmit Packets. This field contains the value of the VLAN tag to be inserted or replaced. The value must only be changed when the transmit lines are inactive or during the initialization phase. Bits[15:13] are the User Priority field, Bit 12 is the CFI/DEI field, and Bits[11:0] are the VID field in the VLAN tag. The following list describes the bits of this field: - Bits[15:13]: User Priority - Bit 12: Canonical Format Indicator (CFI) or Drop Eligible Indicator (DEI) - Bits[11:0]: VLAN Identifier (VID) field of VLAN tag

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCCTRL_INDREG0_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains control information for policing counters associated with MAC_(MSEL)_(AOFF)registers. Offset: 0x9a00704 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:13 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_13: Reserved. RW 0x0 FFAT: Forced Filter fail After Threshold When this bit is set and the corresponding counter crosses the threshold, all the filter events mapped to that counter is treated as fail. Packets are treated as fail from the time threshold is crossed, until the counter is cleared. RW 0x0 FRZ: Freeze Enable This field indicates counter freeze when end of time window is encountered Values: - 0x0: Restarts the counter on next start of time window Free running counter - 0x1: Retains counter value till corresponding counter is read, counts restart from the start of the next time window. RW 0x0 PCM: Policing Counter Mode Values:</b> - 0x0: Free running counter. In this mode, counter is incremented irrespective of time window. - 0x1: Time window-based counter. In this mode, counter is incremented within the provided time window. 9:8 RW 0x0 TWSEL: Time window Select This field is used to select any one of the time window from sbd_pc_tim_wdw_i[3:0] input, for counter increment.
- 00: Time window 0 - 01: Time window 1 - 10: Time window 2 - 11:
Time window 3 7:5 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_5: Reserved. 4:0 RW 0x0 TRSLD: Threshold set for Policing Counter This field indicates programmed filter event count threshold set for the policing counter. - 00000: 1(2^0) - 00001: 2(2^1) - ..... - 11111: 32(2^31) MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCCTRL_INDREG1_0

Packets are treated as fail from the time threshold is crossed, until the counter is cleared. RW 0x0 FRZ: Freeze Enable This field indicates counter freeze when end of time window is encountered Values: - 0x0: Restarts the counter on next start of time window Free running counter - 0x1: Retains counter value till corresponding counter is read, counts restart from the start of the next time window. RW 0x0 PCM: Policing Counter Mode Values:</b> - 0x0: Free running counter. In this mode, counter is incremented irrespective of time window. - 0x1: Time window-based counter. In this mode, counter is incremented within the provided time window. 9:8 RW 0x0 TWSEL: Time window Select This field is used to select any one of the time window from sbd_pc_tim_wdw_i[3:0] input, for counter increment.

RW 0x0 PCM: Policing Counter Mode Values:</b> - 0x0: Free running counter. In this mode, counter is incremented irrespective of time window. - 0x1: Time window-based counter. - 00: Time window 0 - 01: Time window 1 - 10: Time window 2 - 11:
Time window 3 7:5 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_5: Reserved. 4:0 RW 0x0 TRSLD: Threshold set for Policing Counter This field indicates programmed filter event count threshold set for the policing counter. - 00000: 1(2^0) - 00001: 2(2^1) - ..... - 11111: 32(2^31) MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCCTRL_INDREG12_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains control information for policing counters associated with MAC_(MSEL)_(AOFF)registers. Offset: 0x9e00704 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 3 Bit R/W Reset Description 31:13 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_13: Reserved. RW 0x0 FFAT: Forced Filter fail After Threshold When this bit is set and the corresponding counter crosses the threshold, all the filter events mapped to that counter is treated as fail. Packets are treated as fail from the time threshold is crossed, until the counter is cleared. RW 0x0 FRZ: Freeze Enable This field indicates counter freeze when end of time window is encountered Values: - 0x0: Restarts the counter on next start of time window Free running counter - 0x1: Retains counter value till corresponding counter is read, counts restart from the start of the next time window. RW 0x0 PCM: Policing Counter Mode Values:</b> - 0x0: Free running counter. In this mode, counter is incremented irrespective of time window. - 0x1: Time window-based counter. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains control information for policing counters associated with MAC_(MSEL)_(AOFF)registers. Offset: 0xad00704 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:13 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_13: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 FFAT: Forced Filter fail After Threshold When this bit is set and the corresponding counter crosses the threshold, all the filter events mapped to that counter is treated as fail. Packets are treated as fail from the time threshold is crossed, until the counter is cleared. RW 0x0 FRZ: Freeze Enable This field indicates counter freeze when end of time window is encountered Values: - 0x0: Restarts the counter on next start of time window Free running counter - 0x1: Retains counter value till corresponding counter is read, counts restart from the start of the next time window. RW 0x0 PCM: Policing Counter Mode Values:</b> - 0x0: Free running counter. In this mode, counter is incremented irrespective of time window. - 0x1: Time window-based counter. In this mode, counter is incremented within the provided time window. 9:8 RW 0x0 TWSEL: Time window Select This field is used to select any one of the time window from sbd_pc_tim_wdw_i[3:0] input, for counter increment.
- 00: Time window 0 - 01: Time window 1 - 10: Time window 2 - 11:
Time window 3 7:5 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_5: Reserved. 4:0 RW 0x0 TRSLD: Threshold set for Policing Counter This field indicates programmed filter event count threshold set for the policing counter. - 00000: 1(2^0) - 00001: 2(2^1) - ..... - 11111: 32(2^31)

In this mode, counter is incremented within the provided time window. 9:8 RW 0x0 TWSEL: Time window Select This field is used to select any one of the time window from sbd_pc_tim_wdw_i[3:0] input, for counter increment.
- 00: Time window 0 - 01: Time window 1 - 10: Time window 2 - 11:
Time window 3 7:5 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_5: Reserved. 4:0 RW 0x0 TRSLD: Threshold set for Policing Counter This field indicates programmed filter event count threshold set for the policing counter. - 00000: 1(2^0) - 00001: 2(2^1) - ..... - 11111: 32(2^31) MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCSTATUS_DA_INDREG_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains pass status for DA/SA filter where each bit corresponds to specific DA/SA filter event. Offset: 0xba00704 Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit Reset Description 31:0 0x0 FEPS: Filter Event Pass Status When set, indicates at least one DA/SA filter event is passed. Each bit represents individual DA/SA filter event. Access restriction applies. This field is cleared when read (or written to 1 when RCWE bit of MAC_CSR_SW_Ctrl register is set). MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCSTATUS_FILTER_EVENT_INDREG_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains status for the events monitored using policing counters apart from Filter Pass Events which is given below 1. Filter_Fail Offset: 0xbb00704 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:1 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved. RW 0x0 FFAIL: Filter Fail This bit is asserted when "Filter fail" event is set/ triggered at least once. Access restriction applies. This bit is cleared when read (or written to 1 when RCWE bit of MAC_CSR_SW_Ctrl register is set). MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCSTATUS_L3L4_INDREG_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains pass status for L3_L4 filter where each bit corresponds to specific L3_L4 address filter event. Offset: 0xbc00704 Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit Reset Description 31:0 0x0 FEPS: Filter Event Pass Status When set, indicates at least one L3_L4 filter event is passed. Each bit represents individual L3_L4 filter. Access restriction applies. This field is cleared when read (or written to 1 when RCWE bit of MAC_CSR_SW_Ctrl register is set). MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCSTATUS_VLAN_INDREG_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains pass status for VLAN filter where each bit corresponds to specific VLAN filter event. Offset: 0xbd00704 Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 31:0 0x0 FEPS: Filter Event Pass Status When set, indicates at-least one VLAN filter event is passed. Each bit represents individual VLAN filters. Access restriction applies. This field is cleared when read (or written to 1 when RCWE bit of MAC_CSR_SW_Ctrl register is set). MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCNTR_INDREG0_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains count value of policing counters. Offset: 0xbe00704 Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit Reset Description 31:0 0x0 PCNT: Policing Count Value This field contains count value for policing counters. The counter is incremented within the start and end of the time window. Filter events results in increment of count. - Time window mode: When FRZ is not set, the counter value is cleared when start of the time window is detected. When FRZ is set, the counter is cleared on read. - Free running mode: Counter is cleared on read irrespective of the value of the FRZ bit MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_PCNTR_INDREG1_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. This register contains count value of policing counters. 10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description 31:13 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_y: Reserved. 12:8 RW 0x0 PCNUM: Policing Counter Number This field specifies the counter number selected for corresponding VLAN filter event. 7:1 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 PCEN: Policing Counter Enable When set enables the policing counter indicated by the PCNUM field MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_VPCSEL_INDREG1_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0xfe00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE0: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM0: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM0: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM0: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM0: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN0: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM0: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM0: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM0: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM0: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM0: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM0: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN0: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL1_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0xff00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE1: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM1: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM1: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM1: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM1: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN1: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM1: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM1: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM1: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM1: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM1: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM1: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN1: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL10_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10000c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE10: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4DPIM10: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM10: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM10: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM10: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN10: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM10: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM10: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM10: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM10: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM10: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM10: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN10: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL11_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10100c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE11: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM11: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM11: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM11: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM11: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN11: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM11: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM11: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM11: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM11: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM11: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM11: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN11: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL12_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10200c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE12: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM12: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM12: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM12: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM12: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN12: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM12: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM12: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM12: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM12: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM12: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM12: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN12: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. - ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM13: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM13: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM13: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM13: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN13: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL14_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10400c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE14: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM14: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM14: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM14: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM14: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN14: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM14: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM14: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM14: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM14: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM14: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM14: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN14: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10500c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE15: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM15: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM15: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM15: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM15: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN15: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM15: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM15: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM15: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM15: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM15: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM15: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN15: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. - ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
