When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM18: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN18: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL19_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10900c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE19: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4DPIM19: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM19: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM19: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM19: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN19: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM19: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM19: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM19: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM19: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM19: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM19: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN19: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL2_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10a00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE2: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM2: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM2: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM2: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM2: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN2: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM2: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM2: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM2: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM2: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM2: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM2: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN2: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM22: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM22: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM22: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN22: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL23_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10e00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE23: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM23: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM23: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM23: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM23: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN23: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM23: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM23: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM23: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM23: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM23: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM23: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN23: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL24_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x10f00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE24: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4DPIM24: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM24: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM24: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM24: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN24: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM24: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM24: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 4 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM24: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM24: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM24: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN24: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM27: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM27: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM27: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM27: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN27: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.
#### 10.8.5.7 10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5
MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL28_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x11300c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE28: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM28: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM28: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM28: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM28: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN28: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM28: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM28: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM28: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM28: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM28: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM28: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN28: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x11400c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE29: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM29: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM29: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM29: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN29: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM29: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM29: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM29: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM29: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM29: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM29: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. RW 0x0 L3PEN29: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. 10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4DPIM3: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM3: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM3: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM3: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN3: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM3: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. - ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM3: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM3: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. - ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM31: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM31: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM31: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM31: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM31: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN31: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL4_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x11800c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE4: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4DPIM4: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM4: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM4: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM4: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN4: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM4: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM4: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3DAIM4: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM4: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM4: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM4: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN4: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x11900c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000)

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE5: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM5: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM5: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM5: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4SPM5: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN5: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set.

Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN5: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM8: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM8: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM8: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM8: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set. RW 0x0 L3SAM8: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN8: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_L3_L4_CONTROL9_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The Layer 3 and Layer 4 Control register controls the operations of filter 0 of Layer 3 and Layer 4. Offset: 0x11d00c04 Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 DMCHEN: DMA Channel Select Enable. When set, this bit enables the selection of the DMA channel number for the packet that is passed by this L3_L4 filter. The DMA channel is indicated by the DMCHN bits. When the bit is reset, the DMA channel is not decided by the filter. 30:28 RO 0x0 Reserved_30_28: Reserved. 27:24 RW 0x0 DMCHN: DMA Channel Number. When DMCHEN is set high, this field selects the DMA Channel number to which the packet passed by this filter is routed. The width of this field depends on the number of the DMA channels present in your configuration. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE9: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_22: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4DPIM9: Layer 4 Destination Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4DPM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L4DPM9: Layer 4 Destination Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Destination Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Destination Port number field for matching. RW 0x0 L4SPIM9: Layer 4 Source Port Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L4SPM0 bit is set high.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L4SPM9: Layer 4 Source Port Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 4 Source Port number field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 4 Source Port number field for matching. RO 0x0 Reserved_17: Reserved. RW 0x0 L4PEN9: Layer 4 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Source and Destination Port number fields of UDP packets are used for matching. When this bit is reset, the Source and Destination Port number fields of TCP packets are used for matching. The Layer 4 matching is done only when the L4SPM0 or L4DPM0 bit is set. 15:11 RW 0x0 L3HDBM9: Layer 3 IP DA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of higher bits of IP Destination Address that are matched in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: Bits[12:11] of this field correspond to Bits[6:5] of L3HSBM0 which indicate the number of lower bits of IP Source or Destination Address that are masked in the IPv6 packets. The following list describes the concatenated values of the L3HDBM0[1:0] and L3HSBM0 bits: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: LSB[0] is masked. - 2: Two LSBs [1:0] are masked
- ... - 127: All bits except Msb are masked. This field is valid and
applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set. 10:6 RW 0x0 L3HSBM9: Layer 3 IP SA Higher Bits Match. IPv4 Packets: This field contains the number of lower bits of IP Source Address that are masked for matching in the IPv4 packets. The following list describes the values of this field: - 0: No bits are masked. - 1: Lsb[0] is masked - 2: Two Lsbs [1:0] are masked - ... - 31: All bits except Msb are masked. IPv6 Packets: This field contains Bits[4:0] of L3HSBM0. These bits indicate the number of higher bits of IP Source or Destination Address matched in the IPv6 packets. This field is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 or L3SAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAIM9: Layer 3 IP DA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3DAM0 bit is set high. RW 0x0 L3DAM9: Layer 3 IP DA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Destination Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3SAM0 bit because either IPv6 DA or SA can be checked for filtering. RW 0x0 L3SAIM9: Layer 3 IP SA Inverse Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for inverse matching. When this bit reset, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for perfect matching. This bit is valid and applicable only when the L3SAM0 bit is set.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 5 Bit R/W Reset Description RW 0x0 L3SAM9: Layer 3 IP SA Match Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source Address field is enabled for matching. When this bit is reset, the MAC ignores the Layer 3 IP Source Address field for matching. Note: When the L3PEN0 bit is set, you should set either this bit or the L3DAM0 bit because either IPv6 SA or DA can be checked for filtering. RO 0x0 Reserved_1: Reserved. RW 0x0 L3PEN9: Layer 3 Protocol Enable. When this bit is set, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv6 packets. When this bit is reset, the Layer 3 IP Source or Destination Address matching is enabled for IPv4 packets. The Layer 3 matching is done only when the L3SAM0 or L3DAM0 bit is set. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_LAYER3_ADDR0_REG0_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. For IPv4 packets, the Layer 3 Address 0 Register 0 register contains the 32-bit IP Source Address field. For IPv6 packets, it contains Bits[31:0] of the 128-bit IP Source Address or Destination Address field. Offset: 0x11e00c04 Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 31:0 0x0 L3A00: Layer 3 Address 0 Field. When the L3PEN0 and L3SAM0 bits are set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control0 register, this field contains the value to be matched with Bits[31:0] of the IP Source Address field in the IPv6 packets. When the L3PEN0 and L3DAM0 bits are set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control0 register, this field contains the value to be matched with Bits[31:0] of the IP Destination Address field in the IPv6 packets. When the L3PEN0 bit is reset and the L3SAM0 bit is set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control0 register, this field contains the value to be matched with the IP Source Address field in the IPv4 packets. When the L3PEN0 and L3DAM0 bits are set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control4 register, this field contains the value to be matched with Bits[63:32] of the IP Destination Address field in the IPv6 packets. When the L3PEN0 bit is reset and the L3SAM0 bit is set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control4 register, this field contains the value to be matched with the IP Destination Address field in the IPv4 packets. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_MAC_LAYER3_ADDR1_REG5_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. For IPv4 packets, the Layer 3 Address 1 Register 5 register contains the 32-bit IP Destination Address field. For IPv6 packets, it contains Bits[63:32] of the 128-bit IP Source Address or Destination Address field. When the L4PEN0 and L4DPM0 bits are set in MAC_L3_L4_Control26 register, this field contains the value to be matched with the UDP Destination Port Number field in the IPv4 or IPv6 packets. 15:0 0x0 L4SP26: Layer 4 Source Port Number Field. When the L4PEN0 bit is reset and the L4DPM0 bit is set in the MAC_L3_L4_Control26 register, this field contains the value to be matched with the TCP Source Port Number field in the IPv4 or IPv6 packets. When the L4PEN0 and L4DPM0 bits are set in MAC_L3_L4_Control26 register, this field contains the value to be matched with the UDP Source Port Number field in the IPv4 or IPv6 packets. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_HASH_KEY11_8_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The RSS Hash Key to be used in the RSS Hash calculation is 40 bytes. This is organized as 10 registers of 4 bytes each as shown in the following table. You can access these registers indirectly through the MAC_RSS_Data viewport register. 10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 6 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The RSS Hash Key to be used in the RSS Hash calculation is 40 bytes. This is organized as 10 registers of 4 bytes each as shown in the following table. You can access these registers indirectly through the MAC_RSS_Data viewport register. Offset: 0x1c700c8c Read/Write: R/W Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 31:24 0x0 HASHKEY7: Hash Key Byte 7 23:16 0x0 HASHKEY6: Hash Key Byte 6 15:8 0x0 HASHKEY5: Hash Key Byte 5 7:0 0x0 HASHKEY4: Hash Key Byte 4 MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_LOOKUP_DATA0_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The format of each location in the RSS Lookup Table is given in the following table. The size of the table is selected (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, and so on, locations) during RTL configuration. Offset: 0x1c800c8c Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:11 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_11: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHB: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 6 Bit R/W Reset Description RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE: Reserved. RW 0x0 PD: Packet Drop. When set, the MAC receiver drops the current received packet. In other words, the current packet with 2-Tuple or 4-Tuple fields that are used to determine the RSS Hash and generate the lookup of this location-must be dropped by the MAC Receiver. 7:4 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_y: Reserved. 3:0 RW 0x0 DMCH: DMA Channel Number or Receive Channel List Number. This gives the RxDMA Channel Channel or Receive Channel List Number if DMCHLE is exist, which transfers the received packet to the host memory. Selection between DMA Channel number or receive channel list number is decided based on DMCHLE field. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_LOOKUP_DATA1_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The format of each location in the RSS Lookup Table is given in the following table. The size of the table is selected (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, and so on, locations) during RTL configuration. Offset: 0x1de00c8c Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:11 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_11: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHB: Reserved.

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 6 Bit R/W Reset Description RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHLE: Reserved. RW 0x0 PD: Packet Drop. When set, the MAC receiver drops the current received packet. In other words, the current packet with 2-Tuple or 4-Tuple fields that are used to determine the RSS Hash and generate the lookup of this location-must be dropped by the MAC Receiver. 7:4 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_y: Reserved. 3:0 RW 0x0 DMCH: DMA Channel Number or Receive Channel List Number. This gives the RxDMA Channel Channel or Receive Channel List Number if DMCHLE is exist, which transfers the received packet to the host memory. Selection between DMA Channel number or receive channel list number is decided based on DMCHLE field. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_LOOKUP_DATA119_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. In other words, the current packet with 2-Tuple or 4-Tuple fields that are used to determine the RSS Hash and generate the lookup of this location-must be dropped by the MAC Receiver. 7:4 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_y: Reserved. 3:0 RW 0x0 DMCH: DMA Channel Number or Receive Channel List Number. This gives the RxDMA Channel Channel or Receive Channel List Number if DMCHLE is exist, which transfers the received packet to the host memory. Selection between DMA Channel number or receive channel list number is decided based on DMCHLE field. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_LOOKUP_DATA23_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The format of each location in the RSS Lookup Table is given in the following table. The size of the table is selected (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, and so on, locations) during RTL configuration. Offset: 0x1f400c8c Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit R/W Reset Description 31:11 RO 0x0 Reserved_31_11: Reserved. RO 0x0 Reserved_DMCHB: Reserved.

In other words, the current packet with 2-Tuple or 4-Tuple fields that are used to determine the RSS Hash and generate the lookup of this location-must be dropped by the MAC Receiver. 7:4 RO 0x0 Reserved_7_y: Reserved. 3:0 RW 0x0 DMCH: DMA Channel Number or Receive Channel List Number. This gives the RxDMA Channel Channel or Receive Channel List Number if DMCHLE is exist, which transfers the received packet to the host memory. Selection between DMA Channel number or receive channel list number is decided based on DMCHLE field. MGBE_DWCXG_CORE_RSS_LOOKUP_DATA62_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The format of each location in the RSS Lookup Table is given in the following table. The size of the table is selected (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, and so on, locations) during RTL configuration. Offset: 0x21f00c8c Read/Write: See table below Parity Protection: N Shadow: N

MGBE_DWCXG_MTL_DMA_CH0_RXP_ACCEPT_CNT_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The DMA_CH(#i)_RXP_Accept_Cnt registers provide the count of the number of frames accepted by Rx Parser particular to channel. This is an indirect register which can be access through MTL_Indirect_Acc_Control_Status and MTL_Indirect_Acc_Data registers. Offset: 0x248010b4 Read/Write: RO Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 0x0 RXPACOF: Rx-Parser Accept Counter Overflow Bit When set, this bit indicates that the RXPAC Counter field crossed the maximum limit. Access restriction applies. Clears on read. Self-set to 1 on internal event. 30:0 0x0 RXPAC: Rx-Parser Accept Counter This 31-bit counter is incremented whenever the Rx Parser accepts a packet due to AF = 1 and RF = 0. The packet should match the Rx Parser acceptance criteria irrespective of its type (length or type) and size (undersize/runt or oversize). The counter is cleared when the register is read. MGBE_DWCXG_MTL_DMA_CH1_RXP_ACCEPT_CNT_0

10G Ethernet Controller Core Registers cont'd 6 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The DMA_CH(#i)_RXP_Accept_Cnt registers provide the count of the number of frames accepted by Rx Parser particular to channel. This is an indirect register which can be access through MTL_Indirect_Acc_Control_Status and MTL_Indirect_Acc_Data registers. Offset: 0x249010b4 Read/Write: RO Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 0x0 RXPACOF: Rx-Parser Accept Counter Overflow Bit When set, this bit indicates that the RXPAC Counter field crossed the maximum limit. Access restriction applies. Clears on read. Self-set to 1 on internal event. 30:0 0x0 RXPAC: Rx-Parser Accept Counter This 31-bit counter is incremented whenever the Rx Parser accepts a packet due to AF = 1 and RF = 0. The packet should match the Rx Parser acceptance criteria irrespective of its type (length or type) and size (undersize/runt or oversize). The counter is cleared when the register is read. MGBE_DWCXG_MTL_DMA_CH2_RXP_ACCEPT_CNT_0 This register can be accessed only via indirect addressing. The register is added in the spec for informational purpose only. Please refer to databook for details to access this register. The DMA_CH(#i)_RXP_Accept_Cnt registers provide the count of the number of frames accepted by Rx Parser particular to channel. This is an indirect register which can be access through MTL_Indirect_Acc_Control_Status and MTL_Indirect_Acc_Data registers. Offset: 0x24a010b4 Read/Write: RO Parity Protection: N Shadow: N SCR Protection: 0 Reset: 0x00000000 (0b0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000,0000) Bit Reset Description 0x0 RXPACOF: Rx-Parser Accept Counter Overflow Bit When set, this bit indicates that the RXPAC Counter field crossed the maximum limit. Access restriction applies. Clears on read. Self-set to 1 on internal event.
